message:
fix(lipgloss): use round() for center alignment to match Go behavior

Fixes 2 conformance test failures in join_horizontal and join_vertical:

1. join_horizontal_center: Shorter blocks now correctly centered vertically
2. join_vertical_center: Horizontal padding now matches Go's behavior

Root cause: Both functions used .floor() for padding calculation, but Go's
lipgloss uses rounding (which rounds 0.5 away from zero, giving 3 instead
of 2 for center alignment with 5 extra spaces).

Changes:
- join_horizontal line 249: offset calculation uses .round()
- join_vertical line 345: left_pad calculation uses .round()
- Updated test_join_vertical_center_alignment expectations

All 196 lipgloss tests and 229 conformance tests pass.

Fixes: bd-3vqi

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3ecr","title":"E2E scenario: settings toggles + theme switching","description":"Add an end-to-end scenario that validates Settings actually changes the running app.

Scenario outline (headless/custom-IO):
1) Launch demo_showcase in a deterministic mode (fixed seed, animations off).
2) Navigate to Settings.
3) Toggle at least:
   - no-color / ASCII mode (or equivalent)
   - mouse enable (even if headless, ensure toggle state updates)
4) Switch theme preset and confirm global restyling takes effect.
5) Return to Dashboard (or another page) and confirm the changes persist across navigation.

Assertions:
- Output changes in expected ways (e.g., theme name label updates, ANSI sequences absent when no-color).
- No panics and no hangs.

Acceptance:
- Scenario runs deterministically in CI.
- On failure, the artifact bundle clearly shows the last page and the toggle/theme state.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"PurpleCastle","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.112857002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:13:30.343501841Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:13:30.343434255Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-1nwu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.145700883Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.141257332Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-2q4a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.150092478Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.137009755Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ecr","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:59:16.131740545Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3eru","title":"Unit tests: filtering engines (jobs/logs/docs)","description":"Add focused unit tests for filtering/search logic (without spinning up the full UI).

Why:
- Filtering and search are easy to subtly break (case sensitivity, tokenization, pagination interactions).
- We want fast feedback during refactors.

Coverage:
- Jobs filtering/sorting:
  - query matches by name/id/service
  - status filters compose correctly
  - sorting is stable and deterministic
- Logs filtering:
  - level/service selectors work
  - free-text query matches expected fields
- Docs search:
  - match finding and next/prev navigation over a rendered buffer

Edge cases to include:
- empty query
- Unicode input
- very long query (no panic, bounded behavior)
- paste-like input containing newlines/whitespace

Nice-to-have:
- Add a small set of property tests (e.g., filter is idempotent, sorting preserves multiset) if it stays lightweight.

Acceptance:
- Core filter logic is correct and stays correct during refactors.
- Tests are deterministic and run fast.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:09.169792772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:33:26.390328143Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:33:26.390262520Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-1fxb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:26.759858058Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-212j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:25.449597433Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-2lkz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:28.296789159Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3eru","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:34:09.182594415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3gt6","title":"Quality: README section for demo_showcase","description":"Document how to run (and trust) the showcase.

Include:
- `cargo run -p demo_showcase` basics
- flags: theme/seed/no-animations/no-color/no-mouse/no-alt-screen, ssh mode, self-check
- keybindings summary + discoverability notes (help overlay, command palette if present)
- E2E instructions:
  - how to run the smoke tour and where artifacts/logs are written
  - how to run a single scenario for debugging

Also include a *feature coverage matrix* (self-contained):
- For each crate feature we claim (bubbletea/lipgloss/bubbles/glamour/huh/wish/charmed_log/harmonica), list:
  - where it appears in the UI (page/workflow)
  - which automated test(s) cover it (unit/e2e/snapshot)
  - any manual-only checks (mouse drag, SSH interactive)

Acceptance:
- Someone can run the demo and understand it without reading code.
- Someone can validate "everything works" without rediscovering the plan.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:31.297365816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:34:49.959313310Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:34:49.959246495Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3gt6","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:31.956437980Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3gt6","depends_on_id":"bd-1knu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:34.482189415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3gt6","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:31.313191791Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3h5r","title":"Glamour: unit tests and benchmark for LRU cache","description":"# Unit Tests and Benchmark for Glamour LRU Cache

## Purpose
Verify that the new LRU cache implementation is correct and performant.
Include benchmarks to demonstrate the O(1) vs O(n) improvement.

## Test Cases

### 1. test_cache_hit_returns_same_value
Verify that repeated lookups for the same key return cached value.

### 2. test_cache_eviction_at_max_size
Verify that cache evicts oldest entry when MAX_CACHE_SIZE (256) is exceeded.

### 3. test_lru_ordering_correct
Verify that least-recently-used entry is evicted, not arbitrary entry.

### 4. test_cache_key_hash_eq
Verify StyleCacheKey implements Hash + Eq correctly for all field combinations.

### 5. test_cache_miss_converts_and_stores
Verify that cache misses trigger style conversion and store result.

## Benchmark Tests

### 6. bench_cache_hit_performance
Use criterion or test::Bencher to measure cache hit latency.
Target: <100ns per hit (O(1) operation).

### 7. bench_cache_promotion_regression
Compare old Vec::remove approach vs new LRU approach:
- Create 256-entry cache
- Access entry at position 0 (worst case for Vec)
- Old: should be ~256 element shifts
- New: should be ~1 pointer update

### 8. bench_heavy_style_mixing
Simulate real workload: 20 distinct styles accessed in round-robin.
Measure total time for 10,000 accesses.

## Key Files
- crates/glamour/src/syntax.rs (add tests to module)
- crates/glamour/benches/ (create benchmark suite if criterion used)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 5 correctness tests pass
- Benchmark shows >10x improvement for promotion operation
- No regressions in existing glamour tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:28:55.587551638Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T01:31:04.505014347Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T01:31:04.504945398Z","close_reason":"Added 5 unit tests for LRU cache (StyleCacheKey hash/eq, all fields test, hit performance, heavy mixing) and 5 criterion benchmarks (cache_hit, cache_miss, heavy_mixing, lru_promotion, eviction_pressure). All tests passing, benchmarks compiling.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["glamour","performance","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3h5r","depends_on_id":"bd-2oct","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:29:53.551694070Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3h5r","depends_on_id":"bd-2ymx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:28:55.603820169Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":46,"issue_id":"bd-3h5r","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"BENCHMARK SETUP:
If using criterion:
1. Add criterion to glamour dev-dependencies
2. Create crates/glamour/benches/lru_cache.rs
3. Add [[bench]] section to Cargo.toml
4. Run with: cargo bench -p glamour

If using test::Bencher (nightly only):
1. Add #[bench] tests in syntax.rs test module
2. Run with: cargo +nightly bench -p glamour

REGRESSION TEST PATTERN:
Keep the OLD Vec-based implementation in a separate test helper function to compare against. This allows the benchmark to show actual improvement:

fn old_vec_promotion(vec: &mut Vec<Entry>, idx: usize) {
    let entry = vec.remove(idx);
    vec.push(entry);
}

fn new_lru_promotion(cache: &mut LruCache<K,V>, key: &K) {
    cache.get(key); // O(1) promotion built-in
}","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:48:25Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3ilg","title":"E2E Tests: Shell-out and terminal restore","description":"End-to-end tests for terminal release and restore (shell-out).

## Purpose
Test the signature 'drop to shell' feature that releases
the terminal for external commands and restores the TUI.

## Test Requirements

### Diagnostics Pager (D key)
- [ ] Press 'D' triggers pager open
- [ ] Terminal released to pager
- [ ] Diagnostics content displayed
- [ ] Pager exit restores TUI
- [ ] TUI state intact after restore

### Terminal State
- [ ] Alternate screen buffer exit
- [ ] Cursor visibility restored
- [ ] Mouse capture released
- [ ] Raw mode disabled during shell

### Error Handling
- [ ] Pager not found handled gracefully
- [ ] Pager crash doesn't corrupt TUI
- [ ] Ctrl+C during pager handled

### Headless Mode
- [ ] Headless mode skips pager
- [ ] Notification shown instead
- [ ] CI environment detection

## Test Approach
Use PTY to capture terminal state transitions.
Verify screen buffer and mode changes.

## Logging Requirements
- [ ] Log terminal mode before release
- [ ] Log pager command and exit code
- [ ] Log terminal mode after restore
- [ ] Capture screen before/after

## Success Criteria
- Clean terminal release and restore
- No terminal corruption possible
- Works with common pagers (less, more)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:08.607752071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:42:31.878670695Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:42:31.878519583Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","shell-out","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ilg","depends_on_id":"bd-1cgb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:08.642392046Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ilg","depends_on_id":"bd-28kk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:08.861928305Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3ilg","depends_on_id":"bd-t1aq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:45.929961194Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3ut7","title":"bubbletea: Use thread pool for batch command execution","description":"# Task: Replace Unbounded Thread Spawning with Thread Pool

## Problem Statement
Currently, batch commands spawn one OS thread per command:

```rust
// Lines 834-841
for cmd in batch.0 {
    let tx_clone = tx.clone();
    thread::spawn(move || {
        if let Some(msg) = cmd.execute() {
            let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);
        }
    });
}
```

This is problematic because:
1. OS thread creation is expensive (~1ms per thread)
2. Each thread consumes stack space (default 2MB on Linux)
3. No limit on concurrent threads
4. OS may reject thread creation under pressure

## Impact Analysis
A batch of 100 commands:
- Current: Spawns 100 threads, ~100ms overhead, ~200MB stack reservation
- With pool: Reuses N workers, ~1ms overhead, ~2N MB stack usage

## Solution Design

### Option A: Use rayon Thread Pool (RECOMMENDED)
```rust
use rayon::prelude::*;

// In handle_command for BatchMsg:
if let Some(batch) = msg.downcast::<BatchMsg>() {
    let tx_clone = tx.clone();
    rayon::spawn(move || {
        batch.0.into_par_iter().for_each(|cmd| {
            if let Some(msg) = cmd.execute() {
                let _ = tx_clone.send(msg);
            }
        });
    });
}
```

Benefits:
- Work-stealing scheduler for optimal CPU utilization
- Thread pool sized to CPU cores by default
- Battle-tested in production

### Option B: Dedicated Thread Pool
```rust
use std::sync::mpsc;
use std::thread;

struct CommandPool {
    workers: Vec<thread::JoinHandle<()>>,
    sender: mpsc::Sender<Box<dyn FnOnce() + Send>>,
}
```

More control but more code to maintain.

### Option C: Configurable Pool Size
Add configuration option:
```rust
impl Program<M> {
    pub fn with_batch_pool_size(mut self, size: usize) -> Self {
        self.batch_pool_size = Some(size);
        self
    }
}
```

## Implementation Steps
1. Add rayon as optional dependency (feature-gated)
2. Create command pool abstraction
3. Replace thread::spawn with pool submission
4. Add configuration for pool size
5. Add tests verifying bounded thread usage
6. Benchmark batch command performance

## Backward Compatibility
- Default behavior should be similar to current (parallel execution)
- Pool size defaults to num_cpus
- No API changes required

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn batch_commands_use_thread_pool() {
    let active_threads = Arc::new(AtomicUsize::new(0));
    let max_threads = Arc::new(AtomicUsize::new(0));
    
    // Create batch of 100 commands that track thread count
    let commands = (0..100).map(|_| {
        let active = active_threads.clone();
        let max = max_threads.clone();
        Command::new(move || {
            let current = active.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst) + 1;
            max.fetch_max(current, Ordering::SeqCst);
            std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(10));
            active.fetch_sub(1, Ordering::SeqCst);
            None
        })
    }).collect();
    
    let batch = Command::batch(commands);
    // Execute batch...
    
    // Should be bounded to pool size, not 100
    assert!(max_threads.load(Ordering::SeqCst) <= num_cpus::get() * 2);
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Batch commands execute via thread pool
- [ ] Pool size is configurable (default: num_cpus)
- [ ] No thread exhaustion with large batches
- [ ] Performance equal or better than current
- [ ] rayon is optional dependency (feature-gated)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:18:28.393528718Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:18.952146715Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:18.952073849Z","close_reason":"Implemented thread pool for batch command execution. Added rayon as optional dependency behind thread-pool feature flag. Created spawn_batch() helper that dispatches to rayon::spawn (bounded, work-stealing) when enabled, or falls back to thread::spawn. Replaced unbounded thread::spawn in handle_command() batch loop. Added 5 tests: spawn_batch basic execution, many concurrent tasks (200), batch sub-command execution verification (50 commands), bounded parallelism assertion (thread-pool feature), and large batch stress test (500 commands). Fixed 4 pre-existing collapsible_if clippy warnings. All 196 tests pass, clippy clean with -D warnings.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","performance","thread-pool"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ut7","depends_on_id":"bd-2pex","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:18:28.410727754Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3v3x","title":"Demo Showcase: create poison-safe RwLock mechanism","description":"# Create poison-safe RwLock helper or switch to parking_lot

## What
Choose and implement the poison-safety mechanism for RwLock usage across
demo_showcase pages.

**Option A: parking_lot::RwLock (Recommended)**
parking_lot::RwLock never poisons. Switching to it is a mechanical change:
- Add parking_lot to demo_showcase/Cargo.toml
- Replace use std::sync::RwLock with use parking_lot::RwLock
- Remove all .unwrap() calls — parking_lot's read()/write() return guards
  directly (no Result wrapper)

Benefits: simpler API, better performance (no syscall on uncontended), no
poisoning, widely used in async Rust ecosystem.

**Option B: Extension trait**
If adding a dependency is undesirable, create a helper in
demo_showcase/src/util.rs (or similar):

    pub trait RwLockExt<T> {
        fn read_or_recover(&self) -> std::sync::RwLockReadGuard<'_, T>;
        fn write_or_recover(&self) -> std::sync::RwLockWriteGuard<'_, T>;
    }

    impl<T> RwLockExt<T> for std::sync::RwLock<T> {
        fn read_or_recover(&self) -> std::sync::RwLockReadGuard<'_, T> {
            self.read().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())
        }
        fn write_or_recover(&self) -> std::sync::RwLockWriteGuard<'_, T> {
            self.write().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())
        }
    }

Then replace all .read().unwrap() with .read_or_recover() etc.

## Decision Factors
- parking_lot is already used transitively by tokio in the workspace
- parking_lot's RwLock is a drop-in replacement (same API minus Result)
- The extension trait approach preserves std but is more verbose

## Key Files
- crates/demo_showcase/Cargo.toml (if adding parking_lot)
- New or existing utility module for the helper","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:40:14.799666199Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T23:50:43.322399778Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T23:50:43.322333284Z","close_reason":"Implemented parking_lot::RwLock migration","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3v3x","depends_on_id":"bd-ykth","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:40:14.820287983Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":32,"issue_id":"bd-3v3x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DECISION RECOMMENDATION: Use parking_lot::RwLock. Reasons:
1. Already a transitive dependency via tokio
2. Simpler API (no Result wrapper on lock acquisition)
3. Better performance (no syscall on uncontended)
4. Never poisons by design
5. Widely used in async Rust ecosystem (battle-tested)","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:31:00Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-4d5e","title":"E2E scenario: smoke tour (visit every page, no panics)","description":"Scenario: a deterministic smoke tour that proves the app boots, routes, and renders across the whole surface area.

Steps (headless/custom-IO):
- Start app with deterministic Config (fixed seed, animations off).
- Navigate to:
  - Dashboard
  - Jobs
  - Docs
  - Wizard
  - Files
  - Logs
  - Settings
- Open/close the help overlay.
- In Settings:
  - toggle no-color/ASCII mode and confirm ANSI escapes disappear from output
  - switch theme preset and confirm the visible theme indicator updates
- Quit cleanly.

Optional/conditional steps (skip if feature not present yet):
- Open command palette.
- Start guided tour mode.

Assertions:
- No panics and no hangs.
- App state transitions as expected (page headers match).
- Key UI strings appear in output (title, page headers, theme name label).

Acceptance:
- Runs fast and deterministically.
- Catches regressions in routing/layout/theme plumbing early.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:35.237974495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:01:15.574324685Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:01:15.574255606Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-1aad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:23.194963017Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-1nwu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:20.869742832Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-28l3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:13.866362166Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-2q4a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:07:51.168707692Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-35d2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:08.439038707Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:12.230376916Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:10.963093037Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-fdf3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:16.132478669Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:09.690902812Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:18.554874272Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4d5e","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:35.267810523Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-gbps","title":"Unit tests: Config parsing + env/CLI precedence","description":"Add focused unit tests for demo_showcase runtime configuration.

Why:
- The demo will have many toggles; config bugs create confusing UX and flaky tests.
- We want deterministic, documented precedence rules.

Test coverage (minimum):
- Defaults: Config created with no args/env is sane.
- CLI parsing: each flag maps correctly into Config.
- Precedence rules:
  - CLI overrides env where sensible.
  - `NO_COLOR` forces no-color unless explicitly overridden (if we support `--force-color`).
  - `--theme-file` must override `--theme` preset.
- Validation: invalid values (bad seed, bad theme name) produce clean errors.

Acceptance:
- Tests do not require a TTY.
- Failures include actionable assertions/messages.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:12.286603748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:18:13.569277094Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:18:13.569146019Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gbps","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:12.307911148Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-gbps","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:58:12.302950583Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-gp8c","title":"Fix Wish keyboard-interactive auth handling and session manager safety","description":"Fix Wish auth keyboard-interactive flow (russh handler missing), ensure session cleanup task is single-shot, and prevent channel count underflow.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:49:37.934158444Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:49:51.125584226Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:49:51.125513313Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gyzr","title":"Code Quality & Reliability Hardening: Deep Review Findings","description":"# Epic: Code Quality & Reliability Hardening

## Origin
This epic captures all deficiencies identified during a comprehensive deep-dive code review
of the entire charmed_rust codebase conducted on 2026-01-27. The review examined all core
crates (bubbletea, lipgloss, bubbles, glamour, huh, charmed_log, harmonica) for:

- Bugs and correctness issues
- Thread safety and concurrency problems
- Performance inefficiencies
- API design issues
- Code duplication and maintainability

## Scope
The findings span multiple crates with varying severity:
- **CRITICAL**: Issues that can cause panics, data loss, or security problems
- **HIGH**: Issues affecting correctness or significant performance
- **MEDIUM**: Code quality, maintainability, and minor performance issues

## Goals
1. Eliminate all sources of unexpected panics (lock poisoning, bounds checks)
2. Ensure reliable I/O with proper error handling
3. Consolidate duplicated implementations for maintainability
4. Optimize hot paths identified during review
5. Fix all identified bugs and edge cases

## Dependencies
This epic's tasks should be completed before major feature work to ensure a stable foundation.

## Quality Bar
Each fix must:
- Include regression tests where applicable
- Pass all existing tests
- Pass clippy with -D warnings
- Be reviewed for thread safety implications","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:14:47.042212490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:08:44.623313430Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:08:44.623247147Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["code-quality","hardening","review-findings"]}
+{"id":"bd-hqxx","title":"lipgloss: join center alignment off-by-one vs Go","description":"Two conformance tests fail: join_horizontal_center and join_vertical_center. Root cause: Rust calculates left_pad/top_offset directly with floor(), but Go calculates opposite side first. Fix: bottom_offset = floor(extra * (1-factor)), then top_offset = extra - bottom_offset.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AmberDog","created_at":"2026-01-30T03:47:06.431111980Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T03:58:16.157165877Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T03:58:16.157097640Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of bd-3vqi, fixed by AmberDog using round() instead of floor()","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","lipgloss"]}
 {"id":"bd-i0i9","title":"Docs: docs navigation (list of pages + preview)","description":"Implement a docs index + navigation experience.

Design:
- Split view:
  - Left pane: doc page list (filterable list)
  - Right pane: rendered content (glamour + viewport)

Behavior:
- Switching docs is instant.
- Preserve per-doc scroll position when switching away and back (optional but high polish).
- The list should reflect the authored docs content (title/subtitle) rather than raw filenames.

Integration:
- Hooks into in-doc search (search operates within the current doc).

Acceptance:
- Navigation feels fast and "real product" quality.
- Selection and (optionally) scroll state are preserved across page navigations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:43.361710616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:13:42.516077791Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:13:42.516008842Z","close_reason":"Implemented split-view docs navigation with left pane document list and right pane content viewer. Features include: Tab/Arrow keys to switch focus between panes, j/k navigation in list, vim-style scrolling in content, per-document scroll position preservation, and theme-aware rendering with highlighted borders.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","list"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-i0i9","depends_on_id":"bd-22vc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:43.387706158Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-i0i9","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:26.023997036Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ikfq","title":"Unit Tests: bubbletea simulator","description":"Comprehensive tests for the bubbletea test simulator.

## Current State
Simulator has tests but needs expansion for:
- Batch/sequence command execution
- Timer and tick simulation
- Window resize handling
- Error propagation

## Test Requirements

### Simulator Core
- [ ] sim_init() with various Model implementations
- [ ] sim_update() message routing
- [ ] sim_view() output capture
- [ ] Multiple update cycles

### Event Simulation
- [ ] sim_key() for all key types
- [ ] sim_mouse() for all mouse events
- [ ] sim_paste() for clipboard
- [ ] sim_resize() dimension handling

### Command Execution
- [ ] Batch commands execute all
- [ ] Sequence commands maintain order
- [ ] Nested batch/sequence combinations
- [ ] Command result message routing

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Empty batch handling
- [ ] Recursive updates (message spawns message)
- [ ] Large message queues
- [ ] Concurrent timer expirations

## Success Criteria
- Simulator can test any Model without real terminal
- All command types work correctly
- Timing-sensitive tests are deterministic","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:35.290507703Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:38:44.380704888Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:38:42.845133735Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","simulator","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ikfq","depends_on_id":"bd-28co","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:35.310938715Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":28,"issue_id":"bd-ikfq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COMPLETED by VioletDog (2026-01-28)

## Summary
Added comprehensive tests and helper methods to the bubbletea simulator.

## New Helper Methods (5)
- `sim_key(c: char)` - Simulate single character key press
- `sim_key_type(key_type: KeyType)` - Simulate special keys (Enter, Esc, arrows)
- `sim_mouse(x, y, button, action)` - Simulate mouse events
- `sim_resize(width, height)` - Simulate window resize
- `sim_paste(text)` - Simulate bracketed paste

## New Tests (12)
### Event Simulation
- test_sim_key_sends_char
- test_sim_key_type_sends_special_keys
- test_sim_mouse_sends_clicks
- test_sim_resize_sends_dimensions
- test_sim_paste_sends_text

### Sequence Commands
- test_simulator_sequence_command

### Edge Cases
- test_empty_batch_does_not_panic
- test_recursive_updates_bounded
- test_large_message_queue
- test_model_mut_allows_direct_modification
- test_step_without_messages_returns_none
- test_views_accumulate

## Test Results
**22/22 simulator tests pass**","created_at":"2026-01-28T20:38:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iwbw","title":"Dashboard: animated counters/progress (harmonica)","description":"Make the Dashboard feel alive with tasteful, physics-based motion (harmonica).

Why:
- Smooth motion is a signature Charm aesthetic.
- Animated counters and progress bars make metrics changes readable and satisfying.

What to animate:
- Counter values (requests/sec, latency, error rate):
  - spring toward target to avoid jitter
- Progress bars:
  - ease changes rather than snapping
- Optional:
  - a subtle transition when switching focus between dashboard cards

Constraints:
- Must not interfere with input responsiveness.
- Must respect reduce-motion / `--no-animations`:
  - values snap directly to targets

Acceptance:
- Motion is smooth, stable, and never causes flicker.
- The dashboard remains readable even with rapid metric changes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:59.093043228Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:56:24.359362381Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:56:24.359295086Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo_showcase","harmonica"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iwbw","depends_on_id":"bd-2myg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:19.483793101Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-iwbw","depends_on_id":"bd-3q7p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:20.705038373Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-iwbw","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:59.109088230Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
